name: Tamale Instructions
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the corn husks by separating them, removing any pieces of corn silk and debris, placing them in a large bowl filled with tap water, and covering them (using plates to hold down). Let soak for at least half an hour.
2. Rinse the corn husks several times to clean them thoroughly.
3. Drain the corn husks well and dry them off. Separate into piles: big, medium, and too small to use. Also prepare thin strips of husk for tying tamales.
4. To make a tamale:
- Hold a large corn husk with the narrow end pointing right.
- Spread about 2 tablespoons (Tbsp) of dough on the husk, 1-1/2 inches from the wide end and 4 inches from the narrow end.
- Spread dough up to the edge of the husk on one side, leaving a flap for wrapping.
5. Place a spoonful of filling in the middle of the dough.
6. Roll the sides of the corn husk toward the center, enclosing the filling and wrapping the rest around to the back. If the husk isn't wide enough, use another husk to hold the edge closed.
7. Wrap the top (broad end) edge down and flip the bottom (narrow end) up so it covers the broad end's edge.
8. Tie across the middle with thin strips of corn husk to secure the top and bottom flaps.
9. Steam tamales standing upright in a steamer or a metal collander placed in a large pot, ensuring they don't touch water. Boil water and steam for at least an hour (15 minutes recommended).
10. Test if done by easily separating dough from husk; avoid steam burns. Reheat in oven at 350°F for 15 minutes if needed. Frozen tamales can be reheated at 350°F for 30-35 minutes.
